dsa_exam_questions.question: Which data structure is used in recursion?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: Recursive calls are stored in the call stack.

dsa_exam_questions.question: Why is B+ Tree preferred in databases over Binary Search Trees?
options: A. B+ Trees are balanced and disk friendly
B. B+ Trees have no leaf nodes
D. B+ Trees require no memory
E. B+ Trees use more RAM
dsa_exam_questions.correct_answer: A. B+ Trees are balanced and disk friendly
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: B+ Trees reduce disk access and are optimized for database indexing.

dsa_exam_questions.question: Which data structure best suits implementing a priority queue?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: Heaps enable fast access to highest/lowest priority, ideal for priority queues.

dsa_exam_questions.question: Which data structure follows the First In First Out (FIFO) principle?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: A queue follows FIFO order.

dsa_exam_questions.question: Which of the following sorting algorithms is most efficient for large datasets?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: Merge Sort has O(n log n) complexity and is efficient for large datasets.

dsa_exam_questions.question: Which data structure uses LIFO (Last In, First Out) principle?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: A stack uses LIFO order.

dsa_exam_questions.question: Which sorting algorithm partitions data around a pivot and is known for its high average speed but is not stable under all cases?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: Quick Sort is fast on average but unstable.

dsa_exam_questions.question: Which sorting technique is best when the input is almost sorted?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: Insertion Sort works well when the array is already almost sorted.

dsa_exam_questions.question: Which data structure is used in Breadth-First Search (BFS)?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: Breadth-First Search uses a queue to explore nodes level-wise.

dsa_exam_questions.question: Which algorithm uses a priority queue to always expand the least cost node first?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: Uses a priority queue to expand the least-cost node first.

dsa_exam_questions.question: Which tree traversal method is used by a depth-first search (DFS)?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: DFS uses preorder traversal internally.

dsa_exam_questions.question: What is the space complexity of recursive Fibonacci without memoization?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: Recursive Fibonacci without memoization has exponential complexity.

dsa_exam_questions.question: Which hashing technique minimizes clustering in open addressing?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: Reduces clustering better than linear or quadratic probing.

dsa_exam_questions.question: What is the time complexity of heap sort?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: Heap sort has time complexity O(n log n).

dsa_exam_questions.question: Which tree data structure is used in database indexing for maintaining sorted data?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: B-Trees are commonly used for indexing in databases.

dsa_exam_questions.question: Which hashing technique handles collisions using a linked list?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: Chaining handles hash collisions by using linked lists.

dsa_exam_questions.question: Which searching algorithm works efficiently only on sorted arrays?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: Binary Search requires a sorted array for efficiency.

dsa_exam_questions.question: Which data structure is used in Depth First Search (DFS)?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: DFS uses a stack structure to explore nodes deeply.

dsa_exam_questions.question: Which algorithmic technique solves problems by breaking them into smaller subproblems, solving each once, and reusing solutions?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: Dynamic Programming reuses solutions to overlapping subproblems.

dsa_exam_questions.question: Which data structure in Python uses key-value pairs and allows for fast lookup of values using keys?
dsa_exam_questions.explanation: Dictionaries store key-value pairs for fast lookups.
